config.systemPrompt: You are a helpful RAG assistant. Answer questions strictly based on the retrieved context. Be concise and cite sources inline as [n].
You are a helpful RAG assistant. Answer questions strictly based on the retrieved context. Be concise and cite sources inline as [n].

name: Reranker RAG
description: Tuned RAG flow: MMR retrieval (top 20) → LLM-as-reranker via OpenRouter (gpt-4o-mini judge, top 5, score≥0.15) → openai/gpt-4o-mini answer.

config.systemPrompt: You are a grounded retrieval-augmented assistant. Answer ONLY using the provided evidence. Be concise, factual, and cite supporting facts inline as [n] referencing the evidence list.

name: HyDE RAG
description: Hypothetical Document Embeddings: az LLM hipotetikus választ generál a kérdésre, azt embeddeli a kereséshez, majd az eredeti kérdéssel rerankel.

config.systemPrompt: Write a concise hypothetical answer to the user question that would plausibly appear in a relevant document.

config.persona: You are a grounded RAG assistant. Answer only using retrieved evidence.
config.style: Concise, factual, with inline citations like [1].
config.constraints: Refuse to answer if no evidence supports the claim.

config.systemPrompt: Answer ONLY using the provided evidence. Cite inline as [n].

name: Agentic RAG
description: Plan → Act → Observe → Reason → Verify → Iterate: query decomposition, intent routing, dual retrieval (similarity + MMR) with hybrid merge, context compression, agentic LLM reasoning, reflection loop és hallucination guard.

config.persona: You are an autonomous reasoning agent. Think step-by-step, use ONLY the provided evidence, and cite sources inline as [n]. If evidence is insufficient, state it explicitly.
config.style: Chain-of-thought reasoning, concise conclusions, inline citations.
config.constraints: Never hallucinate. If unsure, hedge appropriately. Never fabricate citations.

config.critiquePrompt: Evaluate the answer: Is it fully grounded in evidence? Are all claims cited? Is reasoning coherent? List specific gaps.

name: Self-RAG
description: Az LLM maga dönt a retrieval szükségességéről [Retrieve], értékeli a chunkek relevanciáját [ISREL], majd ellenőrzi, hogy a válasza alátámasztott-e [ISSUP] és hasznos-e [ISUSE] — iteratív önkritikával.

config.routingPrompt: Does the following question require external document retrieval to answer accurately? Answer with 'needs_retrieval' or 'no_retrieval_needed'.

config.systemPrompt: You are a relevance judge. For each retrieved chunk, decide: is it relevant to the user question? Reply with only 'relevant' or 'irrelevant'. Be strict — only mark as relevant if the chunk directly helps answer the question.

config.persona: You are a self-critical RAG assistant. Generate a grounded answer using ONLY the provided evidence. After your answer, internally assess: is every claim supported by the evidence (ISSUP)? Is the answer complete and useful (ISUSE)?
config.style: Factual, concise, with inline citations like [1]. End with a brief self-assessment.
config.constraints: Do not introduce information not present in the evidence. If evidence is insufficient, say so explicitly.

config.checkPrompt: Is every factual claim in the generated answer directly supported by the provided evidence chunks? Score from 0 to 1.

config.critiquePrompt: Is this answer useful, complete, and directly addressing the user's question? If not, identify what is missing or unclear, and produce an improved answer using only the available evidence.

name: Parent-Document Retrieval
description: Kétszintű chunkolás: kis child chunkok indexelése a pontos vektoros kereséséhez, de az LLM a teljes parent chunkot kapja kontextusként — így egyesíti a precíz keresés és a gazdag kontextus előnyeit.

config.persona: You are a grounded RAG assistant. You receive full document sections as context — use them thoroughly to give complete, well-cited answers.
config.style: Detailed yet concise, with inline citations [n].
config.constraints: Base every claim on provided evidence. Do not hallucinate.

name: GraphRAG
description: Knowledge Graph + Vector RAG: az entitásokat és kapcsolatokat gráfban tárolja (Neo4j), query-kor graph traversal + vector keresés párhuzamosan fut, majd hybrid merge egyesíti a gráf-szubgráf kontextust a szemantikus chunk-okkal.

config.persona: You are a knowledge-graph-enhanced RAG assistant. You receive both document chunks and structured knowledge graph triples (entity–relation–entity). Use both sources to give precise, well-connected answers.
config.style: Factual, entity-aware, with inline citations [n]. When graph triples add context, mention the relationship explicitly.
config.constraints: Do not introduce facts absent from provided evidence or graph context.

name: Router RAG
description: A Szakirányító: a Query Router az intent alapján három specializált visszakeresési ösvényre irányítja a kérdést (Factual Direct / Analytical MMR / Broad Hybrid), majd RRF-merge + reranker + LLM generálja a választ.

config.description: Classifies the incoming query into one of three retrieval paths based on intent, complexity and query type.

routes.label: factual
routes.description: Short factual / lookup questions — fast direct similarity search

routes.label: analytical
routes.description: Complex multi-step / comparative questions — query expansion + MMR diversity

routes.label: hybrid
routes.description: Keyword-heavy / terminology-dense questions — sparse+dense hybrid retrieval

config.description: Fast lookup — returns top-5 high-confidence chunks directly
Generates query variants and synonyms to maximise analytical coverage
Sparse-biased hybrid (25% dense / 75% BM25) — excels at exact-term queries
MMR diversity retrieval on expanded queries — covers multiple sub-topics
Reciprocal Rank Fusion across all three retrieval paths — equal weighting by default

config.persona: You are a precise enterprise RAG assistant. The user question was pre-classified and retrieved via the most appropriate path (factual / analytical / hybrid). Answer using only the provided evidence chunks.
config.style: Concise and factual with inline citations like [1].
config.constraints: State which retrieval path was used. Refuse if no evidence supports the claim.

name: Long-Context RAG
description: A Hosszu Memoria: nagy chunkokban (2500 tok) tamogatja a teljes dokumentumok beolvasasat, ingestion-kori LLM-osszefoglalokkal (Redis cache), Lost-in-the-Middle elleni pyramid-rendezesssel, es 200k-kontextus ablaku Claude 3.5 Sonnet generaloval.

config.description: Large semantic chunks preserve paragraph and section coherence

config.systemPrompt: Summarize the following document in 3-5 sentences, capturing the main topics, key facts, and scope. The summary will be used as a context-window header in long-context retrieval.
config.description: Generates a compact per-document summary at ingestion time, stored in Redis and prepended to context at query time
Stores { docId -> summary } mappings. At query time, summaries for retrieved docs are injected at the top of the context window as a table-of-contents.
Pinecone index for large semantic chunks (text-embedding-3-large, 3072-dim)
Expands the user query with synonyms and intent-aware reformulations
Retrieves only top-5 chunks; each chunk is ~2500 tokens (~12500 tokens of evidence total)
Prunes to top-4 large chunks (~10000 tokens) before context assembly
Lost-in-the-Middle mitigation: most-relevant chunks placed at START and END of context window, less-relevant in the middle. Doc summaries injected as preamble. Budget: 60k tokens.
Validates factual grounding before generation; prevents Doc Summarizer artifacts from leaking into the LLM context

config.persona: You are a meticulous long-document analyst. You have been given large document sections plus compact per-document summaries. Use the summaries as a navigation guide and the full sections as your primary evidence.
config.style: Detailed and structured with inline citations like [1]. For multi-part questions, answer each part separately.
config.constraints: Never answer from memory alone. If the provided evidence is insufficient, say so explicitly.

config.description: Claude 3.5 Sonnet: 200k context window. Handles 60k tokens of assembled evidence with ample room.

name: Corrective RAG (C-RAG)
description: A korrekcio alapu RAG: a Relevance Evaluator pontoz minden visszakeresett dokumentumot (CORRECT / AMBIGUOUS / INCORRECT), az Action Selector dont el a tovabblepsrol: helyes -> Knowledge Refiner, ketes -> Refiner + webes kereses, helytelen -> csak webes kereses. Vegul minden forrast osszevon es ellenoriz a generator elott.

config.description: Broad top-10 retrieval — the Relevance Evaluator will filter down to only truly relevant documents

config.systemPrompt: You are a relevance judge. For each retrieved document chunk, score its relevance to the user question as: CORRECT (directly answers), AMBIGUOUS (partially relevant or uncertain), or INCORRECT (irrelevant). Return a JSON array of {chunk_id, score, confidence} objects.
config.description: Scores each retrieved chunk: CORRECT / AMBIGUOUS / INCORRECT. This drives the Action Selector routing.
Decides the corrective action based on Evaluator scores. Routes to one of three paths or combinations.

routes.label: correct
routes.description: All retrieved docs scored CORRECT — pass to Knowledge Refiner only, skip web search

routes.label: ambiguous
routes.description: Some docs AMBIGUOUS — refine existing docs AND supplement with web search

routes.label: incorrect
routes.description: Retrieved docs INCORRECT / no relevant docs — discard, use web search only

config.description: Triggered for AMBIGUOUS or INCORRECT paths. Performs live web search with the original (or rewritten) user query to supplement or replace the retrieved evidence.

Fine-grained knowledge stripping: decomposes each CORRECT/AMBIGUOUS chunk into smaller knowledge strips, scores each strip against the query, and discards strips below the threshold. Only the truly relevant sub-passages survive.
Merges refined internal knowledge (60%) with web search results (40%) using RRF. If only one source is active, full weight goes there.
Final grounding validation — ensures every claim in the selected evidence is traceable to either a refined chunk or a web snippet before generation

config.persona: You are a precise enterprise RAG assistant. Retrieved evidence has been evaluated, corrected, and supplemented (web search if needed). Cite every claim with its source type: [internal] or [web].
config.style: Concise and factual. Distinguish internal knowledge base citations from live web sources.
config.constraints: Never answer from prior knowledge. If sources conflict, explicitly note the discrepancy.

name: Multi-Modal RAG
description: Kep + Szoveg RAG: ket ingestion ag (szoveges dokumentumok + kepek), Vision LLM kepfeliratok generalasahoz (ingestion) es vizualis kerdes-elemzeshez (query-time), kozos vektortarba unifikalt indexelve, GPT-4o generaloval.

config.systemPrompt: Generate a detailed, searchable description of this image. Include: (1) primary subject/scene, (2) all visible text verbatim, (3) chart/table data if present, (4) colors and spatial layout. Format: structured prose for embedding into a vector index.
config.description: Runs at ingestion time. Each image is described as a rich text caption that gets embedded alongside document chunks in the same vector store.
Embeds image captions using the SAME model as text chunks so they land in the same vector space.
Unified Pinecone index holding both text chunks (modality=text) and image captions (modality=image). Metadata includes image_url for generator reference.
Optional: user can attach a single image to their query. The Vision Analyzer converts it to a text description that enriches retrieval.

config.systemPrompt: Analyze the attached image in the context of the user question. Produce a concise text description (max 150 words) suitable for augmenting a semantic search query. Focus on entities, relationships, and visual evidence relevant to the question.
config.description: Runs at query time only when the user attaches an image. Output is merged with the text question to form a richer retrieval query.
Fuses the text question and the Vision Analyzer output (if present) into a single enriched query string for embedding and retrieval. Falls back to text-only when no image is provided.
Retrieves both text chunks AND image captions from the unified index. The modality field in metadata tells the generator which results are image-backed.
Assembles text chunks and image captions into a unified context block. Image entries include their image_url so the generator can reference them. Budget: 5000 tokens.

config.persona: You are a multimodal enterprise assistant. You have access to both text passages and image descriptions from a unified knowledge base. When referencing image evidence, cite the image (e.g., [img-1]) and describe what it shows.
config.style: Clear and factual with inline citations. Distinguish text sources [1] from image sources [img-1].
config.constraints: Only answer from the provided evidence. If an image caption is the sole evidence, state that explicitly.

config.description: GPT-4o: handles text context + optionally sees the original query image directly. Produces grounded answers citing both text and image evidence.

name: Modular RAG
description: Az Epitokeszlet: nem fix architektura, hanem modulok szabad kombinacioja. 5 csereelheto modul: (1) Tobb-forrasu Ingestion, (2) Query Intelligence, (3) Tobb-utvonalu Retrieval, (4) Post-retrieval Processing, (5) Biztonsagos Generacio. Minden modul fueggetlen komponens, barmely elem kivasltjhato anelkul, hogy a tobbi megsertene.

config.systemPrompt: Describe this image in detail: subject, all visible text, charts/tables, spatial layout. Output structured prose suitable for embedding in a vector index.

config.description: Swap: replace with any domain-specific pre-processor (OCR post-correction, HTML stripper, PDF artifact removal, etc.)
Swap: recursive / semantic / sentence / agentic / fixed-size. Each strategy is an independent module.
Swap: Neo4j / Memgraph / Kùzu / NetworkX. Or remove entirely for pure vector flows.
Swap: any embedding model from the registry. Shared by both text chunks and image captions.
Swap: Pinecone / Chroma / Qdrant / Weaviate / pgvector. Unified index for text + image captions.
Swap: intent-based / cost-based / latency-based / round-robin routing strategies.
Stores conversation turns (query+response pairs). Feeds history context back to the router for multi-turn awareness. Swap: in-memory / DynamoDB / Firestore.
Swap: intent-aware / multi-query / step-back / HyDE / none. Feeds Retriever A (similarity path).

config.systemPrompt: Write a concise hypothetical answer that would plausibly appear in a relevant document. Used to generate embedding anchors for MMR retrieval.
config.description: Swap: HyDE / step-back / none. Feeds Retriever B (analytical MMR path).
Path: Factual. Swap: any similarity metric or topK value independently.
Path: Analytical. MMR diversity over HyDE hypothetical docs. Swap: lambda, fetchK, or strategy.
Path: Keyword + Graph. Combines sparse BM25 and dense vector; also receives graph-traversal context. Swap: hybridAlpha freely.
Swap: RRF / linear score blend / pick-best. Each retriever path is an independent module.
Swap: Cohere / Voyage / Jina / LLM-as-reranker. Or remove for latency-sensitive flows.
Swap: extractive / abstractive / pyramid / none. Trims context to LLM token budget.
Swap: presidio / spaCy NER / regex. Or remove for internal-only flows. Last gatekeeper before generation.
Swap: NeMo Guardrails / Llama Guard / custom classifier. Checks assembled context + question before generation.

config.persona: You are a modular enterprise RAG assistant. You receive evidence from multiple retrieval paths (similarity, MMR, hybrid+graph) unified by RRF. Answer precisely with inline citations.
config.style: Structured and factual. Cite every claim as [1], [2], etc. For image evidence, cite as [img-1].
config.constraints: Never answer from prior knowledge. If evidence is insufficient, say so explicitly.

config.description: Swap: any OpenRouter model. The generation module is independent of all upstream modules.
Swap: RAGAS / TruLens / custom LLM judge / none. Verifies every claim traces to retrieved evidence.

config.critiquePrompt: Check: (1) Are all claims grounded in the cited evidence? (2) Is the answer complete for the question? (3) Are citations accurate? Suggest improvements if not.
config.description: Swap: 1-pass / 2-pass / critique-revise / none. Feeds back to LLM Generator for revision.

name: Naive RAG - Pinecone Preset
description: System preset flow with Pinecone vector backend.

config.systemPrompt: You are a helpful RAG assistant. Answer only from retrieved context. If evidence is missing, say so explicitly.

name: Naive RAG - Qdrant Preset
description: System preset flow with Qdrant vector backend.

config.systemPrompt: You are a helpful RAG assistant. Answer only from retrieved context. If evidence is missing, say so explicitly.

name: Naive RAG - Weaviate Preset
description: System preset flow with Weaviate vector backend.

config.systemPrompt: You are a helpful RAG assistant. Answer only from retrieved context. If evidence is missing, say so explicitly.

name: Naive RAG - Chroma Preset
description: System preset flow with Chroma vector backend.

config.systemPrompt: You are a helpful RAG assistant. Answer only from retrieved context. If evidence is missing, say so explicitly.

name: Naive RAG - Supabase Preset
description: System preset flow with Supabase vector backend.

config.systemPrompt: You are a helpful RAG assistant. Answer only from retrieved context. If evidence is missing, say so explicitly.

name: Safety-First RAG Preset
description: Preset flow with PII redaction + guardrails + hallucination guard.

config.rejectionMessage: This request cannot be processed due to policy restrictions.

config.systemPrompt: Answer from retrieved context only. If context is insufficient, explicitly say you do not have enough evidence.

config.rejectionMessage: I cannot answer this reliably from the available evidence.
